text: So guys, we have come a long way in 100 days of code and now we are going to see what are the methods of the list with which we can manipulate the list Let's go to the computer screen and let's get started Intro Now we are going to see the list methods that what are the methods of the list and we will understand how the list is manipulated So suppose I have a list here I write l is equal to 1, 2 let us say 4 and then 6 I will print l, the list will be printed you all know this But if I append l. and then write 7 So what is append? It is a method of the list It is a method of the list which at the end of this list will keep 7 So if I print l now then my original list has been changed and now 7 is also there at the end So what we can do is we can add the element at the end of the list with the help of append method Okay? There are many such methods of our list Let's see them one by one If I run l.sort here and I do one thing I put 11 at the start and then put 45 What I will do is I will run all the methods one by one If I put l.sort here like this, l.sort I have not put a comma here I will put a comma If I do l.sort, then it will be sorted in my list 1, 2, 4, 6, 11, 45 So what will happen here It will be sorted in my increasing order and ascending order If I do reverse is equal to true then it will be sorted in my descending order So you can see here First is the biggest, then the smallest and so on Okay? So this is how we can do it with the help of sort method So you can see here I have written this thing After this, there is a reverse method Okay? l.reverse What does l.reverse do? It reverses our original list That's about it Okay? So what does it do? It reverses a list Okay? It changes the original list Now l.index is a method If I write l.index and I write 1 So it will give me the index of 1 in the list which is 2 Okay? So I have not printed here Actually, I should print this because it returns a value to me and what will this value be? It will be 2 See here, this is 2 value 1 is on index 2 If I put one more 1 at the end then what will it give me? It will give me 2 only which is the index of first occurrence Okay? So it is written here This method returns the index of the first occurrence of the list item Okay? So this is one method Now let's see what count method does Okay? What does count method do? If I want to count how many times 1 is coming Okay? So I can count 2 times are coming in this list So 2 is seen here If I put one more 1 then 3 will come here because 1 is coming in this list 3 times Okay? So you will see 3 here This was count method After this, there is a copy method But to explain the copy method I want to show you one thing If I do something like this I say m is equal to l Now I do m0 is equal to 0 Okay? And after this I print l So if you are coming from any other programming language Or you will try to evaluate and manipulate it in very layman terms You will say that l is a list I printed it This will be printed Very good Then you will say that I made another list named m The content in it is as much as l That is, the content in l will come in m And after that, now because m has become a new list I am telling you how a beginner will think I am telling you this Okay? Keep in mind It is right or wrong We will talk later m is equal to l Someone will think like this And then from m0 is equal to 0 He will think that I will change in the list of m And l will remain like this And expect that this thing will be printed again But to your surprise Now see My original list has changed So what happens here When we do m is equal to l So m is a reference of l Okay? The list is the same The list that is made in the memory is the same So now if you do m is equal to l here So And after that you will change m So l will also change Because you have made a reference And such things are not recommended at all in the Python program You don't do Until and unless you know what you are doing Okay? If you are a beginner Don't make a new list like this You use the copy function So if I write m is equal to l.copy So what will it do? It will make a copy of the list And now that thing will happen What you were expecting That m is equal to 0 So only m will change We did not change l So l will remain the same So see here The same thing happened Okay? I hope you understood this thing I have already told you the append method I will tell you an extend method Okay? But before that I will tell you the insert method If you want to insert in a given index Then you can do it Okay? I will show you how So I will comment out this thing And what will I do here? I will write l.insert I want to insert 899 on one index So the index of 899 in the new list will be 1 Okay? So see 0,1 So in this way it will insert That the index of 899 in the list will be 1 Okay? I hope this is clear Okay? So here you can see That by doing l.insert 1,899 899 will come after 11 Because only then its index will be 1 Okay? I hope you understood this thing Last but not the least Why am I saying this? Last but not the least You can use the extend method There are many methods of list Okay? You I mean even today I find some methods Of some data type In python I say Oh wow! This also exists So you will always learn new things But what I am telling you here The things that are written in the notes All of them You people Always remember Always understand this Because that is the most important And mostly used Okay? Great With that said I will make a new list I say m is equal to 900, 1000 and 1100 Assume I made this list Now I say l.extend And I say m So this means Open m And put it at the end of l This is the simple meaning of this Okay? I will show you I will run it You will understand what I am saying Open l Bring the values inside it And Open m and put it at the end of l Open m and put it at the end of l It is simple It is very simple There is nothing confusing 900, 1000 and 1100 will append at the end of this By extending Okay? Now if you want to join two lists And you want to concatenate There is another way Assume I want to make a k with l and m And I say k is equal to l plus m So the elements of both these lists Will get concatenated If I want l to not change By extending l.extend My l will change But if I want a new list to be made Merging l and m Then I can do that by saying k is equal to l plus m Now if I print k Then what will happen? If I print k Then l and m will get concatenated Will merge Okay? So by doing k is equal to l plus m You see That this thing is getting printed here Okay? So by doing k is equal to l plus m L and m will merge And the new list will be made with k Okay?
